# VVIP Operations Checklist: Pepper Minton

## Gold Tier Protocol -- SnackStack Technologies

---

**Visitor:** Pepper Minton, CTO, SnackStack Technologies
**VVIP Tier:** Gold
**Briefing Date:** TBD
**Location:** Raleigh Executive Briefing Center
**Room:** Developer Collaboration Room (whiteboard-first setup, NOT Executive Suite)
**Executive Sponsor:** Carlos Diaz, VP Developer Experience
**EBC Coordinator:** Assigned at scheduling

---

## CRITICAL OPERATING RULES

> **RULE 1:** No PowerPoint. No slide decks. No marketing materials. If it has a product logo wall, it does not enter the room. Pepper has walked out of briefings that started with slides.
>
> **RULE 2:** Whiteboards everywhere. Fresh dry-erase and wet-erase markers at every station. Replace markers each break.
>
> **RULE 3:** Fast-paced format. Time blocks are 25-45 minutes. Do not let any session run long without explicit approval from Pepper.
>
> **RULE 4:** Standing meeting options must be available. Adjustable-height desks or standing room at every whiteboard station.
>
> **RULE 5:** Carlos Diaz drop-in at 4:00 PM is non-negotiable. Peer-to-peer engineering chat, coffee format.

---

## Pre-Briefing (T-14 days to T-1 day)

### Stakeholder Notifications (T-14 days)

- [ ] **Notify Executive Sponsor** -- Carlos Diaz (cdiaz@redhat.com)
  - Confirm sponsor drop-in at 4:00 PM (coffee format, peer-to-peer)
  - Brief on March 14 incident and post-incident trust-rebuilding goals
  - Emphasize: no slides, no talking points cards visible
  - Confirm Carlos's calendar hold
- [ ] **Notify Account Team** -- Confirm engineering presenters (not product marketing)
- [ ] **Notify EBC Operations** -- Submit Gold VVIP briefing request with explicit "whiteboard-first" room requirement
- [ ] **Notify Incident Response Lead** -- Confirm the on-call engineer who led the March 14 incident is available to present

### Room & Facilities (T-7 days)

- [ ] **Book Developer Collaboration Room** (NOT Executive Suite) -- Full day, 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM
- [ ] **Verify room setup:**
  - Minimum 3 whiteboards on wheels (one per session)
  - Fresh dry-erase AND wet-erase markers (both types required)
  - Standing desk options and adjustable-height workstations
  - Laptop station for hands-on chaos engineering lab
  - AV system available but DEPRIORITIZED (do not warm up projector unless requested)
  - Power outlets at every seat and standing station
- [ ] **Book backup chaos engineering cluster** -- In case Pepper breaks the primary one
- [ ] **Confirm developer lounge access** for informal breakout conversations

### Catering (T-7 days)

- [ ] **Dietary preferences confirmed:** No restrictions, but Austin-style food preferred
- [ ] **Beverage preferences confirmed:** Oat milk lattes, cold brew, agua fresca, sparkling water
- [ ] **Submit catering order:**
  - 9:00 AM -- Breakfast tacos (variety: egg & potato, chorizo, veggie, bean & cheese), oat milk lattes, cold brew, fresh fruit
  - 10:30 AM -- Break refresh: oat milk lattes, sparkling water, light snacks
  - 12:00 PM -- Lunch taco spread (different menu: brisket, al pastor, fish, veggie), horchata, agua fresca
  - 2:30 PM -- Afternoon refresh: cold brew, oat milk, sparkling water, Austin-made snacks
  - 4:00 PM -- Coffee service for sponsor drop-in (cold brew for Pepper, whatever Carlos prefers)
- [ ] **Confirm catering vendor** can source breakfast tacos (not bagels)
- [ ] **Verify oat milk is available** -- Pepper specifically asked about this in pre-visit survey

### Welcome & Materials (T-3 days)

- [ ] **Personalized welcome display** -- Configured for lobby screen and collaboration room entry
  - Format: "Welcome, Pepper Minton, SnackStack Technologies"
  - Include SnackStack logo (approved version)
  - Keep it simple -- Pepper dislikes over-produced welcome displays
- [ ] **NO print materials packet** -- Pepper will not read it
  - EXCEPTION: Print the incident timeline and tape it to the wall for the 9:30 AM session
  - EXCEPTION: Print sample YAML configurations as handouts for auto-scaling session
- [ ] **Prepare digital materials** -- GitHub repo link with all architecture diagrams, sample configs, lab environment access
- [ ] **Prepare printed architecture diagrams** as handouts ONLY (not presentations)

### Presenter Preparation (T-3 days)

- [ ] **Distribute presenter briefing packets** with explicit instructions
- [ ] **Brief all presenters on communication style:**
  - NO slides. If they bring slides, they will be asked to leave.
  - Whiteboard-first. Every session starts at a whiteboard, not a screen.
  - Direct, fast-paced, candid. No corporate polish.
  - Pepper will ask hard technical questions. Deep expertise required.
  - Acknowledge uncertainty honestly -- "I don't know, let me find out" is the right answer
- [ ] **Conduct dry run** for incident retrospective session (highest risk)
- [ ] **Confirm chaos engineering lab environment** is tested and broken (intentionally, so she can fix it)
- [ ] **Verify engineering presenters are shipping engineers, not product marketing**

### Gift (T-3 days)

- [ ] **Select Gold tier-appropriate gift**
  - Recommend: engineering-themed (high-quality mechanical keyboard, vintage computing book, artisan coffee subscription)
  - DO NOT GIVE: branded swag, plush items, anything with a logo wall
- [ ] **Gift wrapping and presentation card** prepared
- [ ] **Confirm gift is available** on briefing day

### Final Confirmation (T-1 day)

- [ ] **Send agenda to Pepper** (24-hour lead time -- Pepper prefers short lead time)
- [ ] **Confirm Pepper's travel arrangements** (flight from Austin)
- [ ] **Verify all room bookings** are still active
- [ ] **Verify catering order** with taco vendor specifically
- [ ] **Test chaos engineering lab environment** -- intentionally break things and verify recovery
- [ ] **Confirm presenter availability** -- all confirmed with backups identified
- [ ] **Verify backup cluster** is ready for lab session
- [ ] **Confirm incident response engineer** is attending in person

---

## Day-of Operations

### Pre-Arrival (8:00 AM - 9:00 AM)

- [ ] **EBC Manager arrives by 8:00 AM** -- Room walkthrough and final setup check
- [ ] **Verify welcome display** is active on lobby screen and collaboration room entry
- [ ] **Verify all whiteboards** are clean and have fresh markers
- [ ] **Verify catering setup** -- Breakfast tacos in place, lattes brewing
- [ ] **Do NOT set up projector** -- Keep AV dormant
- [ ] **Confirm first presenter** (incident response engineer) is on-site

### Lobby Greeting (8:45 AM - 9:00 AM)

- [ ] **EBC Manager positions at lobby** by 8:45 AM
- [ ] **Greet Pepper by name** at lobby entrance (casual, not formal)
- [ ] **Escort to Developer Collaboration Room** -- Quick walk-through of whiteboards and lab setup
- [ ] **Offer beverage on arrival** -- Oat milk latte or cold brew
- [ ] **Introduce to the incident response engineer** immediately (she will want to meet them first)

### During Briefing

- [ ] **Monitor catering timing:**
  - 10:30 AM -- Break refresh delivered (quick in and out, do not interrupt whiteboard flow)
  - 12:00 PM -- Lunch buffet ready in room (no separate dining room)
  - 2:30 PM -- Afternoon refresh delivered
  - 4:00 PM -- Coffee service for sponsor drop-in
- [ ] **Sponsor drop-in at 4:00 PM:**
  - [ ] Text Carlos Diaz at 3:45 PM to confirm he is en route
  - [ ] Ensure cold brew and Carlos's preferred drink are ready
  - [ ] DO NOT print talking points for Carlos to hold -- brief him verbally before entry
- [ ] **Monitor session timing** -- Keep blocks short; Pepper will signal if something runs too long
- [ ] **Be available for ad-hoc requests** -- Marker refills, whiteboard wipes, lab reset
- [ ] **Lab session (2:30 PM):**
  - [ ] Confirm primary cluster is running
  - [ ] Confirm backup cluster credentials accessible
  - [ ] Solutions Architect is ready at 2:20 PM

### Gift Presentation (4:45 PM -- during wrap-up)

- [ ] **Present gift** casually during wrap-up
- [ ] **Short handwritten note** from EBC Manager and Carlos Diaz

### Departure

- [ ] **EBC Manager escorts Pepper to lobby** at conclusion
- [ ] **Confirm workshop scheduling** (the 2-day Austin workshop commitment)
- [ ] **Thank Pepper for her time and for the public case study advocacy**

---

## Post-Briefing (T+1 day to T+14 days)

### Immediate Follow-up (T+1 day)

- [ ] **Send thank-you note** -- Short, direct, engineering-tone (NOT marketing language)
  - From Carlos Diaz (sponsor) AND EBC Manager
  - Reference specific whiteboard discussions
- [ ] **Send architecture diagrams** that were created during the briefing
- [ ] **Log briefing in engagement history** -- CRM entry with full session notes

### Commitment Tracking (T+2 days)

- [ ] **Document all commitments** made during briefing:
  - Auto-scaling architecture review document (close overdue CAB item)
  - 2-day scaling workshop in Austin (schedule within 14 days)
  - Smart kitchen edge computing proposal timeline
  - Any new commitments from chaos engineering lab
- [ ] **Send commitment summary to Pepper** (short, bullet list, NO fluff)
- [ ] **Create follow-up tasks in CRM** for each commitment

### Engagement Updates (T+7 days)

- [ ] **Update engagement score** in CRM (target: 78 to 82+)
- [ ] **Update relationship stage** assessment
- [ ] **Brief Carlos Diaz** on outcomes and next steps
- [ ] **Schedule next touchpoint** with Pepper (within 2 weeks)

### NPS & Feedback (T+3 days)

- [ ] **Send NPS survey** to Pepper (short survey, not long form)
- [ ] **Target score:** 8/10 or higher (post-incident baseline)
- [ ] **Log feedback** and distribute to presenter team

### Pipeline Updates (T+7 days)

- [ ] **Update opportunity stages** in CRM:
  - Smart Kitchen Edge Platform ($1.8M) -- update based on briefing outcome
  - Auto-Scaling Advanced Tier ($480K) -- update based on workshop conversion
- [ ] **Schedule follow-up meetings** for advanced opportunities

---

## Protocol Reference: Gold VVIP Tier

| Element | Requirement | Status |
|---------|-------------|--------|
| Room | Developer Collaboration Room (whiteboard-first) | Required |
| Lobby Greeting | EBC Manager meets at lobby | Required |
| Catering | Austin-style (tacos, oat milk lattes, cold brew) | Required |
| Sponsor Drop-in | Required -- Carlos Diaz, coffee format, peer-to-peer | Required |
| CEO Drop-in | NOT RECOMMENDED -- Pepper prefers peer engineering chats | Not Scheduled |
| Gift | Engineering-themed, no branded swag | Required |
| Welcome Display | Personalized, understated | Required |
| Thank-you Note | From Sponsor + EBC Manager | Required |
| Materials | Digital-first, minimal print (exceptions noted) | Required |
